summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the jsPDF output('dataurlnewwindow') function generates HTML containing the injected XSS payload. A malicious filename breaks out of the <iframe src=""> attribute via an unescaped double-quote, injecting a <script> tag that executes arbitrary JavaScript in the new window context.
expected_security_impact: The output('dataurlnewwindow', { filename: maliciousValue }) call should generate HTML via document.write() where the malicious filename breaks out of the iframe src attribute and injects a <script> tag containing the proof string.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built a Puppeteer-enabled Docker image, loaded the jsPDF UMD bundle, called output('dataurlnewwindow') with a crafted filename, and captured the generated HTML. The HTML contains the XSS proof string confirming successful attribute breakout and script injection.
verification_method: The exploit script uses Puppeteer to load jsPDF in a headless browser, overrides window.open to capture the document.write() HTML, and checks for the presence of the proof string EVOHUNT_XSS_PROOF_2ac0469e in the captured HTML. This distinguishes a real injection from a benign filename containing special characters.
evidence_produced_by_run: The document.write() HTML output contains the injected <script> tag with the proof string.
The double-quote in the filename successfully broke out of the <iframe src=""> attribute.
The generated HTML structure confirms the attribute breakout: <iframe src="data:application/pdf;filename="> followed by </iframe><script>...</script>.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
